column4: PII blur on task / profile photos
column7: Runs after B-44 in the same BullMQ pipeline. Azure Computer Vision detects visible text regions (phone numbers, emails, addresses, ABNs). Sharp.js applies Gaussian blur over flagged regions before CDN serving. Prevents taskers and posters from bypassing the platform via contact info visible in photos.
For completion proof: extract EXIF DateTimeOriginal + GPS via ExifTool.
Post-launch. Vision model checks completion photo plausibly matches task category.
Magic-byte type validation. Max 10MB photos. Sharp.js server-side resize.
Detects: synthetic identity, collusion, platform-leakage. Flags taskers with frequent cancellations — surfaced in admin portal moderation queue.

Per-user Redis quotas. AI extraction 20/day. Auth: 10 attempts / 15min. Redis middleware + config; shared pattern across endpoints.
PII scrubbed before any user content goes to LLM.
No UPDATE/DELETE. Retention: 7 years (ATO).
BullMQ fan-out: new task/message/photo/review → relevant checks. B-37 notification dispatcher already establishes the fan-out pattern; this reuses it.

column4: TFN encryption at rest (AES + Azure Key Vault)
column7: [MDR §5.2] Privacy Act + ATO requirement. TFN stored AES-encrypted via Azure Key Vault. Controlled-decrypt path only — TFN never logged, never returned in API responses. Migration job for any existing unencrypted records.

[MDR §5.2] ATO requirement. Cron job runs 1 July each year. Aggregates all completed task earnings per contractor for the financial year. Generates PDF statement stored in Azure Blob. Download link emailed via Resend. Mobile screen M-233 provides in-app access.

column4: Payment lifecycle edge cases (dispute hold + release + payout-ready gate)
column7: [MDR §5.4] Three gaps: (1) held_indefinitely reserve state on dispute open — locks payout until dispute resolves, prevents premature transfer to tasker. (2) Auto-release reserve on dispute closure — B-31 triggers Stripe transfer after outcome recorded. (3) PAYOUT_READY gate on task completion — blocks completion if tasker Connect account not charges_enabled/payouts_enabled, shows hard error with deep-link to Connect onboarding (ID 44).

[MDR §5.5] BullMQ job evaluates thresholds after each dispute resolution, no-show, review reveal, and fraud score update. Triggers: 3 disputes lost, 3 no-shows, rating < 3.0 with ≥10 reviews, fraud_score > 0.7. Auto-sets account suspended + logs reason code. Notifies tasker via push + email. Feeds reinstatement queue (AP-58).

[MDR §5.9] Nightly BullMQ cron evaluates retention rules per data class: financial records (7yr archive→purge), thread messages (7yr), KYC docs (7yr post-closure → Azure Blob delete), PCC docs (90d post-approval → purge, retain SHA-256 hash), profile data (deletion+30d → anonymise), analytics events (2yr → delete). Each run logs deleted record counts to audit log (B-52). Deletion vs anonymisation logic per class. Privacy Act compliance.

column4: Geographic supply check + demand signal capture
column7: [MDR §5.3] On task post: PostGIS query checks contractor count within radius for category. Zero result → returns soft-block response to mobile (M-234). Waitlist signup stored in demand_signals table (suburb, category, user_id, timestamp). Nightly aggregate summary available to admin — feeds AP-55 Demand Map post-launch. Demand signals also generated on failed task searches and filter-with-no-results events.
Search by name, email, phone, or ABN. Full-text + exact match. AdminDataTable component shared with AP-14, AP-24, AP-28.
Complete profile: personal info, trust tier, KYC, job history, Stripe Connect status.

Payload inspection per failed event. Error log + retry history per entry. One-click re-enqueue. Manual resolve with mandatory reason capture. Filter by event type, age, status. PagerDuty alert wired for payment-class DLQ entries (Blueprint §12).
Queue depth, active jobs, failed jobs. Alert threshold for queue depth >500.

Immutable log of every admin action. Search + filter by actor, target, action type, time range. CSV export. Tamper-evident display with hash chain (Blueprint §27 + SRS NFR-SEC-008). Before/after diff view and monthly partition optimisation are POST.

TOTP MFA mandatory. Three roles: Super Admin (full access) + Sub Admin (limited — no destructive actions, no financial overrides) + FSE Role (field sales agent — feature access controlled by Super Admin). Role permissions configurable per FSE user by Super Admin. Session timeout 4h idle.

column4: FSE Admin Role — field sales portal access
column7: Field Sales Executive login to admin portal under FSE Role. Super Admin toggles which features are visible per FSE user. Default FSE access: verification queue (submit/track manual KYC for in-person onboarding), their own referred tasker list, commission tracking (pending/earned), and basic lead notes. No access to platform financials, bulk actions, or destructive operations. Replaces the separate FSE Flutter app — field agents use the same admin portal on mobile browser.
Post-launch. AI-curated taxonomy — not needed for MVP operations.
Post-launch. AI template generation + A/B testing — deferred.
Post-launch. AI-augmented trust rules — manual config sufficient for MVP.

Blueprint §31. 8 daily reports auto-emailed 7am AEST: Operations Summary, Payment, Dispute, KYC (incl. manual queue + FSE-onboarded), Webhook Health, AI Cost, Fraud Alert, Field Sales. Weekly: marketplace health, contractor performance, geographic heatmap, AI accuracy, promo performance, cohort LTV. Monthly: financial close, RCTI batch, ATO prep, compliance summary, investor metrics. Each report = templated SQL + SendGrid email; BullMQ cron handles scheduling.
Blueprint §31. Self-service: pick entity, fields, filters. NL query → parameterised SQL via LLM. AI router (AI-01) built; UI is a form + table renderer.
Blueprint §32. First screen after login. 24 tiles across 6 sections. REST polling (60s/30s intervals) — no WebSocket. Tile data sourced from existing APIs; frontend is grid layout + polling hook.
Post-launch. Unified AI suggestion inbox — deferred with 3.10.
Post-launch. Demand heatmap needs 3–6 months of data to be meaningful.
Sub Admin initiates → Super Admin approves. Covers: refunds >$1,000, account hard delete, bulk operations >50 records. FSE role cannot initiate maker-checker actions. Audit log captures both actions.

[MDR §5.2] ATO SERR requirement for AU gig platforms. Aggregates contractor earnings, ABNs, TFNs per financial year. Exports in ATO-specified format. Due each July — infrastructure must be ready at launch even though first report is July 2027. Separate from RCTI export (AP-20).

[MDR §5.5] Queue of auto-suspended taskers awaiting review. Tasker submits remediation plan via mobile app. Senior Admin reviews + approves/denies reinstatement. Full audit trail: suspension reason, remediation plan, decision, approver identity. Separate from manual suspend/ban flow (AP-04).

[MDR §2.3] Admin create/edit/deactivate promo codes. Fields: code string, discount type (% or flat), discount value, usage limit (total + per-user), expiry date, category targeting (all or specific categories), min task value. Usage counter shown per code. B-17 backend validation engine already built — this is the admin UI + create/update endpoints only. AI-design workflow (Blueprint §30) deferred POST.

column4: Override: stuck draft task — manual edit/publish
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. Admin can edit fields on a stuck draft task and force-publish, or delete it. Triggered from job detail view. Reason code + audit log required. POST — edge case volume low at launch.

column4: Override: open task 0 bids >48h — broadcast / lower budget / expire
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. Three admin actions on a stale open task: (1) re-broadcast to wider radius, (2) prompt poster to lower budget, (3) force-expire with full refund. Each action logged with reason code. POST — ops can manually handle at launch volume.

column4: Override: assigned task with unreachable contractor
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. When contractor goes silent after assignment: (1) reassign to next-ranked bidder, (2) cancel + full refund to poster, (3) force-complete with admin note. Audit trail records which action and why. POST — rare edge case at launch.

column4: Override: in-progress task with no completion signal
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. Admin marks task complete on behalf of tasker when GPS check-in exists but completion not triggered. Requires internal note with min 50 chars. Triggers normal payment release flow. POST — manual workaround acceptable at launch.

column4: Override: bid submitted with poster inactive
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. Two options: (1) auto-reject bid and notify tasker, (2) override-accept and notify poster to confirm. Triggered from bid detail view. POST — infrequent at launch volume.

[MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. When PaymentIntent auth expires before capture: (1) request re-auth from poster, (2) manual refund if poster unreachable. Links to AP-17 refund flow. POST — Stripe auth window (7d) gives ops time to handle manually.

column4: Override: capture failed — retry / switch method / notify
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. When Stripe capture fails: (1) retry immediately, (2) request poster update payment method, (3) notify poster and hold payout. Each action creates audit log entry. POST — Stripe retry logic handles most cases automatically.

[MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. When tasker Stripe Connect onboarding stalls >30d: (1) force-close Connect account, (2) initiate manual bank transfer for outstanding balance, (3) notify tasker. POST — Connect completion rate high; rare edge case.

[MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. When Stripe refund API call fails: (1) retry, (2) switch to alternative method, (3) create manual ledger entry for offline resolution. Full audit trail with Stripe error code captured. POST — Stripe refund failure rate negligible.

[MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. When AI mediator proposal goes unacknowledged >7d: (1) force-accept AI resolution, (2) escalate to human arbitration queue (AP-24/26). Admin records reason. POST — deferred with AI mediation complexity.

column4: Override: locked thread needs reopen for dispute
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. Message threads auto-lock after completion. Admin can unlock for dispute evidence collection. Unlock logged with reason; thread re-locks after dispute closes. POST — AP-26 manual arbitration handles this manually at launch.

column4: Override: user locked out — manual ID verify + reset
column7: [MDR §2.6] Blueprint §28. When account locked due to failed auth attempts or fraud flag: admin triggers manual ID verification step, then resets lock. Separate from suspend/ban (AP-04) — this is account recovery not punishment. POST — low volume; ops can use AP-04 flow as workaround at launch.

[MDR §2.11] Graph UI showing entity relationships: users, devices, IPs, cards, bank accounts, addresses. Edges: signup-from, paid-with, shared-device, hired-by. Velocity anomaly overlay. Fraud case actions (suspend, escalate, review) from graph nodes. POST — auto-suspend (B-57) handles launch-volume fraud; graph viz needed at scale. Needs 3-6 months of signal data to be meaningful.
Breakpoints: mobile/tablet/desktop. Affects ~20 key screens. Flutter LayoutBuilder handles most of this; effort is the breakpoint config + testing on key screens.
Replace mobile bottom nav with persistent sidebar/topbar at ≥900px. NavigationRail widget handles the sidebar; FW-01 breakpoints already established.
Map Flutter routes to URL paths. go_router handles URL sync cleanly. Enables browser back/forward + bookmarking.
Intercept browser back during checkout or form fill. Show 'Leave page?' confirmation.
Dynamic document.title per route. Favicon + apple-touch-icon set.
Web Push API via service worker. VAPID key pair, browser permission prompt.
navigator.geolocation for GPS check-in. Requires HTTPS.
getUserMedia for camera; HTML file input for gallery/doc upload.
Google/Apple sign-in uses redirect_uri on web. Separate OAuth client config.
flutter_stripe SDK injects Stripe.js on web. Test all three payment modes.
Validate real-time chat, bid notifications, job status updates work in browser.
navigator.share(). Fallback to clipboard copy on desktop.
Required for Australian Privacy Act + Stripe / GA4 tracking cookies.
Tab order, focus indicators, ARIA labels. Screen reader testing required.

column4: PWA manifest + service worker + install prompt
column7: Enables 'Add to Home Screen'. Flutter generates manifest.json; service worker caches app shell. Standard template — minimal custom logic.

column4: Flutter web build pipeline + CDN hosting
column7: flutter build web --release with CanvasKit. Azure Static Web Apps or Vercel. GitHub Actions step + CDN config — not complex code.
Chrome, Firefox, Safari (WebKit), Edge. Flutter web has known Safari quirks.
Inject basic og:title / og:description in index.html per route for shareable pages.

column4: Next.js project setup + App Router + hosting
column7: Next.js 14 App Router. TypeScript. Tailwind CSS. Vercel hosting with ISR support.
Critical architectural decision. Shared cookie auth so logged-in users flow between apps.
Hero, how-it-works, category grid, testimonials, app store CTA. SSR for personalisation.
/hire-a-[category]. ISR revalidation on category update webhook.
/hire-a-[category]-[suburb]. Could be 5,000–20,000 pages. 24h ISR. Same template as SEO-04 — scale doesn't add dev hours; effort is DB query + ISR config.
/taskers/[slug]. JSON-LD Person + Review schema for rich snippets. Schema template reused from SEO-14; page data from existing tasker profile API.
Poster + tasker journey explained. Static page.
Platform fee % breakdown. Config-driven so fee changes don't need code deploy.
Trust tier explainer. WWCC, PCC badges explained.
Markdown-based help articles. AP-37 admin CMS feeds this via API. SSG with ISR; same pipeline as other static pages.
Post-launch. Requires content production investment.
Smart App Banner meta tag + branch.io SDK. Static page; meta tag injected in layout.tsx.
About, ToS, Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy. Legal review required.
LocalBusiness, Service, Person + Review, FAQPage schemas. JSON-LD is templated per page type; schema.org definitions are reusable across SEO pages.
Dynamic og:title / og:description / og:image per page.
Auto-generated covering all pages. Split into sitemap index for >50k URLs.
Disallow /app/*. Canonical tags on all programmatic pages.
LCP <2.5s, CLS <0.1, FID/INP <100ms. Lighthouse CI in build pipeline.
Gated behind cookie consent. Server-side events via Conversions API.
Post-launch. Vercel Edge Config or GrowthBook. Needs baseline traffic first.
Abstraction over Gemini Flash / Claude Haiku / Claude Sonnet / GPT-4o. Single interface.
Prompt versioning, latency, token cost, error rates. Ship before any AI feature goes live. SDK integration + dashboard config; heavy on setup not code.
Prompts as versioned artefacts in DB. Golden dataset eval script. Precision/recall tracked per version via Langfuse (AI-02 built). Effort is golden dataset curation + eval runner.
JSON schema validation on all structured LLM outputs. Zod validators shared across endpoints. Base validator is reusable — effort is per-endpoint schema definition.
Postgres-backed feature table. Pre-computed features per tasker. Nightly refresh.
Post-launch. Needs ~500 completed tasks before model is meaningful.
LLM generates SEO-friendly slug, meta description, FAQ block from task description.
Post-launch. LLM suggests win-rate improvements. Build after 1,000+ bids. Feature flag to activate.
Post-launch. Needs 6+ months of data.
Post-launch. Needs 3–4 months of activity data.
Post-launch. Needs real LTV data from completed cohorts.

[MDR §3.5] Per-user daily quota (e.g., 20 AI extractions/day) enforced via Redis counters. Org-wide daily budget cap with alert thresholds at 70 / 85 / 95% spend. PagerDuty alert fires at 95% — prevents runaway AI bill. LLM router (AI-01) already routes to cheapest model per task class; this adds the quota gate and alerting layer. SRS NFR-COST compliance. (make it minimal like keep a count in tabe)
Already covered in B - 44, if we need to monitor profile image and all we can consider else we dont need this.
On PR: lint + tests. On merge: Docker → ACR → staging. On tag: prod. Fastlane for mobile.
Three tiers. DB migrations in staging first. Secrets per environment via Key Vault. This is runbook + pipeline YAML config — DEV-01 CI/CD already handles most of the mechanics.
Flyway or Prisma Migrate. Expand-contract pattern. Zero-downtime migrations.
All secrets in Azure Key Vault. Injected via managed identity. Standard Azure config — audit log is automatic with Key Vault.
Post-launch. Run before major releases once real traffic baselines are established.
RTO: 1h. RPO: 15min. Postgres WAL archiving + daily snapshots. DR runbook tested quarterly.
Azure Monitor + Application Insights. PagerDuty on-call. 99.9% = 8.7h downtime/year.

column4: Log aggregation + structured logging + PII redaction
column7: Structured JSON logging across all services. PII redaction (email, phone, TFN) before log storage — Privacy Act compliance requirement, cannot ship to prod without this. Log aggregation pipeline. 90d hot / 1yr cold retention. Moved from POST: PII log redaction is a launch blocker per Privacy Act obligations.

[MDR §4.1] Terraform modules for all Azure resources: PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Redis Cache, Blob Storage (with lifecycle policies), App Service Plan + slots, Static Web Apps, Container Registry, Key Vault, Application Insights, Virtual Network + subnets. Environment promotion via Terraform workspaces (dev/staging/prod). State stored in Azure Blob backend with locking. Enables full environment rebuild from scratch for DR.

column4: Network edge (Azure Front Door + WAF + API Management)
column7: [MDR §4.2] Azure Front Door: global load balancing, SSL termination, CDN for static assets, health probes. WAF policy: OWASP Core Rule Set 3.2, custom rules for AU-only traffic patterns. Azure API Management: rate limiting, API versioning, request logging, subscription keys for future third-party integrations. Required for Privacy Act compliance and Stripe marketplace security expectations.

column4: Compute topology (BullMQ worker pool + scaling + sticky sessions)
column7: [MDR §4.3] Separate Azure App Service for BullMQ workers (job processing isolated from API traffic — prevents queue saturation starving API responses). Horizontal scaling rules for both API and worker plans (CPU/memory thresholds). Azure Application Gateway sticky sessions for Socket.IO (required for real-time messaging across multi-instance deployments). All configured via DEV-09 Terraform modules.

[MDR §4.5] Sentry SDK integrated into Flutter mobile, React admin, and Node.js backend. PII scrubbing rules configured (email, phone, name, TFN redacted from error payloads before transmission to Sentry) — required for Privacy Act compliance. Source maps uploaded for readable stack traces. Alert routing: critical errors → PagerDuty, warnings → Slack.

column4: Customer-managed keys (CMK) for sensitive document storage
column7: [MDR §4.4] Azure Blob Storage containers holding KYC documents (ID scans, selfies), TFN records, and PCC/WWCC uploads configured with customer-managed encryption keys via Azure Key Vault. CMK rotation policy (annual). Separate from standard Key Vault secret management (DEV-04). Privacy Act + ATO requirement for highest-sensitivity data classes.
